data.title: Forgent Power Solutions IPO Terms Could Push Market Cap Above $8.8 Billion
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

Forgent Power Solutions unveiled terms of a planned initial public offering that could give the maker of electrical equipment used in data centers an initial market capitalization topping $8.8 billion.

Forgent on Monday said it plans to sell nearly 16.6 million shares at between $25 and $29 apiece in the IPO.

At the $27 midpoint of that range, the Dayton, Minn., company said it expects net proceeds of about $427.7 million, or roughly $491.8 million if the underwriters exercise an option to buy an additional 2.49 million shares.

Forgent said investment firm Neos Partners, its current owner, plans to sell another 39.4 million shares in the offering, bringing the size of the IPO to 56 million shares.

Forgent said it would have more than 304.4 million shares outstanding after the IPO, assuming an exercise of the overallotment option, for a market capitalization of around $8.83 billion at the $29-a-share pricing high end.

Forgent said it has applied to list its shares on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol FPS.

San Diego-based Neos, which built Forgent through the acquisitions of MGM Transformers, PwrQ, States Manufacturing and VanTran, will still hold about 80% of the company's voting power after the IPO.

Neos, which invests in North American companies that provide products and services to the renewable energy, power grid, infrastructure, data center and energy-intensive industrial sectors, manages $2.7 billion of capital.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: Netflix Stock Is on a Nightmare Run. Why It Just Got an Upgrade. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Netflix has taken a battering ever since the video streamer said it had agreed to buy Warner Bros. — but the beaten-down stock caught an upgrade on Monday.

Helena Wang, analyst at the Singaporean brokerage and wealth manager PhillipCapital, raised her rating on Netflix to Accumulate from Sell and upped her price target to $100 from $95. The new target implies that shares can rise 16% from their level as of Friday's close.

"Netflix continues to demonstrate clear leadership in the video on-demand space and strong pricing power," Wang wrote in a research note. "Volatility is expected due to the Warner Bros. deal, but we believe Netflix is well-positioned structurally and financially for long-term growth."

The analyst said that Netflix's broadcast of upcoming live events like the World Baseball Classic in Japan will "add juice to their business," and noted that the streamer forecast in its fourth-quarter earnings report that advertising revenue will double over the current fiscal year.

Netflix stock climbed 0.9% to $86.88 ahead of Monday's opening bell. Futures tracking the benchmark S&P 500 were flat as investors fretted that the "sell America" trade could accelerate after President Donald Trump threatened Canada with further tariffs.

The shares have slumped 17% since Dec. 5, when Netflix said it had agreed to buy Warner Bros.'s streaming and studios assets for $27.75 a share, with cable being spun out to investors. Wall Street is worried Netflix will have to take on too much debt to get the deal down, particularly now it has made its offer all-cash to fend off a hostile bid from Paramount.

Netflix's fourth-quarter earnings failed to end the slump. The streamer beat Wall Street's profit and revenue targets but issued weaker-than-expected margin guidance, as it expects to ramp up content spending this year and incur some expenses from the Warner takeover.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1440 GMT on Monday:

** The potential sale of Brazilian aluminium producer Companhia Brasileira de Aluminio is moving forward, although discussions with Emirates Global Aluminium have slowed, according to two people familiar with the matter.

** Century Aluminum <CENX.O> will take a 40% stake in Emirates Global Aluminium's project to build the first primary aluminium smelter in the U.S. in almost 50 years, the companies said.

** Quantum computing firm IonQ <IONQ.N> said it would buy chipmaker SkyWater Technology <SKYT.O> for about $1.8 billion, in a deal that would bring semiconductor manufacturing in-house and boost the development of its next-generation quantum processors.

** Abu Dhabi state energy company XRG said it is buying an additional 7.6% stake in two liquefied natural gas trains at the Rio Grande LNG project in Texas, deepening its investment in one of the world's largest export facilities.

** Hungary's MOL <MOLB.BU> agreed to pay up to 1 billion euros ($1.19 billion) to buy a majority stake in Serbia's oil firm NIS <NIIS.BEL> from its Russian owners, Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said .

** Zijin Gold <2259.HK> will buy Canada's Allied Gold <AAUC.TO> for about C$5.5 billion ($4.02 billion) in cash, the companies said, as the Chinese miner ramps up its global expansion against the backdrop of record high prices of the yellow metal.

** Leidos Holdings <LDOS.N> said it would buy power design firm ENTRUST Solutions Group from private equity firm Kohlberg for about $2.4 billion, as it looks to expand engineering offerings for utility customers.

** Private equity firm CVC <CVC.AS> has agreed to buy U.S.-based credit manager Marathon for up to $1.2 billion, it said, in the latest deal in the fast-consolidating private credit market.

** Shares of Spire Healthcare surged as much as 20% after the British private hospital group confirmed over the weekend it was in preliminary talks with multiple buyout firms including Bridgepoint and Triton to "explore strategic options".

** Swiss private bank EFG International <EFGN.S> said it would acquire Quilvest Switzerland Ltd, a Swiss private bank with longstanding ties to Latin America.

** Czech investor Daniel Kretinsky's EP Group said it planned to launch a voluntary offer to take control of Fnac Darty , valuing it at 1.1 billion euros ($1.3 billion), sending shares of the French retailer 18% higher .

** Merck <MRK.N> is no longer in discussions to buy cancer drug developer Revolution Medicines <RVMD.O>, the Wall Street Journal reported on Sunday.

data.title: CoreWeave Stock Soars. Why Nvidia Is Investing $2 Billion in the AI Cloud Company. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Artificial-intelligence cloud company CoreWeave surged early Monday on news of an expanded partnership with Nvidia, which includes a $2 billion investment from the chip maker.

Nvidia invested in CoreWeave class A common stock at a purchase price of $87.20 per share. CoreWeave shares were up 10.8% at $103 in premarket trading.

The two companies said the agreement will enable CoreWeave to accelerate the buildout of more than five gigawatts of "AI factories" by 2030.

"CoreWeave's deep AI factory expertise, platform software, and unmatched execution velocity are recognized across the industry. Together, we're racing to meet extraordinary demand for NVIDIA AI factories — the foundation of the AI industrial revolution," Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said in a statement.

CoreWeave is the largest of the so-called "neoclouds" which provide cloud-computing capacity for AI and has built a revenue backlog of $55.6 billion through deals with OpenAI, Meta Platforms, and others. It has close ties to Nvidia, which provides access to advanced hardware.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

data.title: Canadian Rail Freight Volumes Nudge Up Despite Drop in U.S. Volumes — Market Talk
data.description: Canadian rail freight volumes are holding up despite waning U.S. cargoes. Cargo volumes carried by Canadian railways was up slightly in November, rising 0.5% on-year to 31.4 million metric tons, which is on par with the five-year average, Statistics Canada data shows. The rise was driven by increased non-intermodal and intermodel traffic, offsetting a 10th consecutive monthly fall in loadings from connections with American railways. American freight, down 11% on-year to 3 million tons, was the lowest for a November in five years. The data shows domestic non-intermodal trading edged up 0.1% on-year, led by a 12.8% increase in carloadings of wheat after Canada's wheat harvest hit a record high. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

EDMONTON, AB / ACCESS Newswire / January 26, 2026 / Innovotech Inc. , a leading contract research organization providing proprietary antimicrobial testing and advanced laboratory services for regulated industries, today announced that it will participate in the DealFlow Discovery Conference, taking place January 28-29, 2026.

Craig Milne, CEO of Innovotech Inc. will deliver a company presentation and be available for one-on-one investor meetings throughout the event. Learn more about Innovotech at www.innovotech.ca.

Event Details:

  • 3rd Annual DealFlow Discovery Conference

  • The Borgata Hotel, Casino & Spa

  • Atlantic City, NJ

Investors interested in scheduling a meeting with the Innovotech Inc management team should request an investor pass to attend the conference (no cost to attend).

About Innovotech:

Innovotech Inc. is a Canadian boutique contract research organization specializing in advanced laboratory services and commercializing proprietary technologies. A leader in biofilm science, the company provides expertise in antimicrobial testing, pharmaceutical analysis, and industrial laboratory solutions, supported by innovative methodologies and products including its widely-used MBEC Assay® kit for high-throughput antimicrobial and antibiotic testing. Innovotech serves clients across multiple sectors, delivering customized research and testing services designed to meet rigorous regulatory and scientific standards such as those of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and Health Canada through its ISO-certified and GMP-accredited laboratories. data.title: Trump Administration's USA Rare Earth Investment to be Paid in Milestones — Market Talk
data.description: The Trump administration's capital injection into USA Rare Earth--both the $277 million in direct funding and a $1.3 billion loan--will be paid in milestones largely through 2028, with a tail piece into 2029, CFO Rob Steele tells analysts on a call. CEO Barbara Humpton adds that the milestone payments are intended to reduce taxpayers' exposure to the business. "As we achieve milestones, taxpayer support continues to build," Humpton says. "But the American taxpayer is never overcommitted on this plan." USA Rare Earth jumps 20% in early trading. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: EU Says WhatsApp Must Obey Digital Services Act's Tougher Rules
data.description:
By Edith Hancock

The European Union will tighten its oversight over Meta Platforms' WhatsApp, saying that the messaging service has enough European users to warrant stricter oversight under its content moderation rules.

The European Commission said that WhatsApp is now considered a Very Large Online Platform under the Digital Services Act, saying its "channels" feature crosses the EU executive's user thresholds for such a classification. The DSA obliges entities labeled as VLOPs to do more to protect users from illegal and harmful content.

WhatsApp was not immediately available for comment.

Write to Edith Hancock at edith.hancock@wsj.com

data.title: Apple gains; J.P.Morgan raises PT on hopes of strong iPhone sales
data.description:
** Shares of Apple rise 1.8% to $252.61

** J.P.Morgan raises PT to $315 from $305, implying an upside of ~27% to stock's last close

** Brokerage expects iPhone sales in Q1 of 2026 "to track ahead of consensus expectations," forecasts 16% iPhone revenue growth to put the tech major on track for "highest-ever iPhone revenue growth since Sep-21"

** JPM sees limited margin pressure from higher memory costs because company's long-term supplier contracts and scale help "drive materially favorable component cost outcomes relative to IT hardware peers"

** Average rating of 48 analysts is "buy", median PT is $288 - data compiled by LSEG

** Shares up 8.6% in 2025

data.title: SkyWater Technology jumps on IONQ's $1.8 billion takeover offer
data.description:
** Shares of chipmaker SkyWater Technology surge 7.3% to $33.48 in morning trading

** IONQ says it has signed a deal to buy SKYT for about $1.8 billion

** Shares of IONQ jump 4.3% at $49.17

** SKYT shareholders will receive $15 in cash and $20 in IONQ stock for each share of SkyWater stock

** The deal is expected to close in the second or third quarter of 2026, after which SKYT will operate as a wholly owned subsidiary, remaining under the leadership of CEO Thomas Sonderman, who will report to IonQ CEO Niccolo de Masi

** In 2025, SKYT gained ~32%, while IONQ was up ~7%

data.title: Canada - January 26
data.description:
The following are the top stories from selected Canadian newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

THE GLOBE AND MAIL

- Airbus has no regrets about taking over Bombardier's C Series jet programme and is focused on making it profitable, the plane maker's newly appointed Canada chief said, as the company weighs when to move ahead with a larger version of the aircraft.

- BDO Canada said it is acquiring funding advisory firm GrantMatch, as the national business consultancy looks to help companies secure government funding while they seek support to expand into new markets.

NATIONAL POST

- U.S President Donald Trump criticised Canada's trade ties with China, saying Ottawa was "systematically destroying itself", while Prime Minister Mark Carney said the agreement in question does not breach the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement.

Notes:

1.

Rule 8 of the Takeover Code (the "Code") sets out the circumstances in which Dealing Disclosures and/or Opening Position Disclosures are required to be made by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them, persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more and exempt principal traders ("EPTs") connected with the parties to an offer.

2.

Where an offeror is marked "Disclosure of dealings and positions in this offeror is not required", dealings and positions in relevant securities of that offeror are not required to be disclosed under Rule 8. This is either because it has been announced that the offer or possible offer is, or is likely to be, solely in cash or because disclosure is not required for some other reason.

3.

Dealings and positions required to be disclosed under Rule 8 of the Code include dealings and positions in derivatives referenced to, and options in respect of, relevant securities of an offeree company and, where appropriate, an offeror (see Note 2 above).

4.

The information set out in the Disclosure Table relating to the relevant securities in issue of an offeree company or of an offeror (and any amendments thereto) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by such company under Rule 2.9 of the Code. Similarly, details of any deadline set under Rule 2.6 by which a potential offeror must announce either a firm intention to make a bid or that it does not intend to make an offer (or, in the case of Rule 2.6(a), by which the Panel must consent to an extension of the deadline) has been extracted from the relevant announcement made by the offeree company.

5.

Additions and deletions of offeree companies and offerors are reflected in the Disclosure Table throughout the day, following the relevant announcement. Other amendments may also be made throughout the day. In any event, details of the classes of relevant securities issued by an offeree or offeror company, the number of such securities in issue ("NSI") and the International Securities Identification Number ("ISIN") of such securities announced under Rule 2.9 of the Code before 5.00 pm each day will be included in the final version of the Disclosure Table by 5.30 pm that day. The final version of the Disclosure Table issued each day will be marked "Final" and persons required to make Dealing Disclosures and Opening Position Disclosures are advised to use that final version. Any changes to the Rule 2.6 deadline of a potential offeror will also be reflected in the "Final" version of the Disclosure Table, although any such changes will not be shown in the "Amendments" section at the top of the Disclosure Table.

6.

Dealing Disclosures by the parties to an offer, persons acting in concert with them and connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the business day (as defined in the Code) following the date of the relevant dealing and Dealing Disclosures by persons with an interest in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by 3.30 pm on the business day following the date of the relevant dealing.

7.

Opening Position Disclosures by the parties to an offer and by connected EPTs must be made by no later than 12 noon on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Where required, Opening Position Disclosures by persons with interests in relevant securities of 1% or more must be made by no later than 3.30 pm on the 10th business day after the commencement of the offer period or the announcement that first identifies an offeror (as the case may be). Details of the time and date at which an offer period commenced and at which an offeror was first identified are set out in the Disclosure Table. In order to calculate the deadline for Opening Position Disclosures, it is therefore necessary to count forward 10 business days from the relevant date.

8.

Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service ("RIS").

9.

This Note is applicable if it says "See Note 9 below" by the name of the company in the Disclosure Table above.  The Executive has granted the company a dispensation from the requirements under the Code that announcements must be published via a RIS. The company is instead required to publish all announcements on its website. The Executive has also granted a dispensation from Note 3 on Rule 8 of the Code that disclosures made under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a RIS. Therefore, Opening Position Disclosures and Dealing Disclosures required under Rule 8 of the Code may be made to the company at the address stated in the announcement that commenced the offer period.

data.title: Blackstone, Yanmar Mull Bids for Majority Stake in VW's Everllence, Bloomberg Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Blackstone and Japanese industrial equipment maker Yanmar Holdings are among possible bidders to buy a majority stake in Volkswagen's Everllence unit, Bloomberg reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The unit--which makes ship engines and power-plant turbines--could be worth 5 billion euros to 6 billion euros ($5.94 billion to $7.12 billion), Bloomberg reports. It says there is no certainty any of the possible bidders will proceed with offers for Everllence.

Representatives for Blackstone, VW and Yanmar declined to comment to Bloomberg.

Full story: https://tinyurl.com/47ydzmte

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: Gold miners climb as price breaks $5,100 barrier
data.description:
** Shares of gold miners rise, as bullion prices soar above the record $5,100-per-ounce level for the first time

** Spot gold up 1.9% to $5,077.24 per ounce, after earlier touching an all-time high of $5,110.50

** Prices surge as investors sought a safe haven away from international political tension, with silver and platinum also scaling all-time highs

** Top miners Newmont and Barrick Mining rise 1.4% and ~3%, respectively

** South African miners: Gold Fields gains ~6%, AngloGold Ashanti adds 3.5% and Sibanye Stillwater , climbs 2.4%

** Canadian miners: Agnico Eagle Mines and Kinross Gold gain 2.3% and ~3%, respectively
